programme.title: Thappad
programme.desc: Amrita, a young woman, leads a content life with her husband, Vikram. However, their happiness is shattered when he slaps her at an office party causing her to file for a divorce.

programme.title: Black & White
programme.desc: A suicide bomber befriends a professor in India to sneak into Independence Day celebrations, aiming to execute his deadly mission while grappling with unexpected emotions.

programme.title: Tumbbad
programme.desc: In a mythological tale, a goddess faces unforeseen consequences when humans construct a temple for her firstborn. The story explores how their actions impact both the divine and mortal realms.

programme.title: Auron Mein Kaha Dum Tha
programme.desc: Krishna and Vasudha fledgling relationship is interrupted when Krishna gets involved in a murder. After spending 23 years in prison, Krishna is released, leading to one final meeting with Vasudha.

programme.title: Janhit Mein Jaari
programme.desc: A small-town girl starts selling condoms to raise awareness, challenging deep-rooted taboos while balancing family expectations and personal conviction.

programme.title: Crew
programme.desc: Three dedicated women find themselves tangled in unexpected situations, navigating a tricky web of lies that challenges their friendships and tests their resilience.

programme.title: Mukkabaaz
programme.desc: Shravan Singh dreams of boxing glory but clashes with his rival's niece's love. Now, ambition and romance collide inside and outside the ring with dangerous consequences.

programme.title: Traffic Signal
programme.desc: Film depicts lives around a Mumbai traffic signal, showing struggles and stories.

programme.title: Waah Zindagi
programme.desc: A poor farmer's son walks on a difficult path to become a successful businessman and win over his childhood crush's heart.

programme.title: Sherdil - The Pilibhit Saga
programme.desc: In Pilibhit Tiger Reserve, people left elders for tigers seeking compensation.

programme.title: Suraj Pe Mangal Bhari
programme.desc: Suraj discovers Madhu rejects brides for him.

programme.title: Kanjoos Makhichoos
programme.desc: Man saves money for father's Char Dham Yatra dream, tragedy strikes in floods.

programme.title: Tarla
programme.desc: Explores Tarla Dalal's journey and legacy as an iconoclast during the early liberalisation period, empowering women through the art of cooking.

programme.title: Saina
programme.desc: The inspiring journey of a badminton legend who rises from humble roots to international acclaim, overcoming challenges with sheer discipline.

programme.title: Mrs. Chatterjee vs Norway
programme.desc: Debika Chatterjee, an Indian woman living in Norway, battles to regain custody of her children after Norwegian authorities remove them from her care, fighting against legal and cultural challenges.

programme.title: Chhatriwali
programme.desc: Sanya, a chemistry whiz without a job, aims to educate young minds about sex, breaking taboos while seeking opportunities to share her expertise.

programme.title: Tehran
programme.desc: An intense spy thriller follows agents navigating political conspiracies, danger and betrayal as they race against time to prevent global chaos.

programme.title: Hotel Mumbai
programme.desc: A gripping true story of courage during Mumbai's 2008 Taj Hotel siege, highlighting hotel staff, a couple's sacrifices, and selfless acts to protect guests and loved ones.

programme.title: U-Turn
programme.desc: Mysterious deaths occur on a flyover after illegal U-turns, and journalist-intern Radhika investigates. However, a twist makes her the prime suspect, forcing her to clear her name.

programme.title: The Last Color
programme.desc: 9-year-old Choti wishes to attend school but she faces struggle for daily survival. She befriends a widow who lives a life of abstinence. She later becomes an advocate and fights for societal reforms.

programme.title: Joggers Park
programme.desc: Jenny, a confident single woman, lives life on her own terms. Everything changes when a retired judge unexpectedly falls for her and enters her world.

programme.title: Iqbal
programme.desc: A deaf and mute boy dreams of playing cricket for India, battling social stigma and physical limitations with determination and a mentor's support.

programme.title: Rock On 2
programme.desc: Old friends reunite to revive their music band, confronting unresolved issues and personal struggles as they seek redemption through melodies.

programme.title: Dhadak
programme.desc: Young lovers from different social strata defy family and tradition to be together, leading to a tragic journey that challenges their innocence and emotional resolve.

programme.title: Uri: The Surgical Strike
programme.desc: India's military retaliates against a terrorist attack with surgical precision, showcasing valor, strategy, and patriotic spirit in a gripping narrative.

programme.title: Firaaq
programme.desc: After the 2002 Gujarat riots, survivors, perpetrators, and silent witnesses face lasting scars, forcing them to confront the way tragedy reshapes lives, hearts, and entire communities forever.

programme.title: Mr. & Mrs. Mahi
programme.desc: A former cricketer turned coach, played by Rajkummar Rao, helps his wife, Janhvi Kapoor, achieve stardom in the sport, but struggles to cope with her rising fame and his own fading glory.

programme.title: Yudhra
programme.desc: Yudhra seeks revenge on a cartel for his parents' murder. He infiltrates the group, but uncovers a shocking truth about his own history. This discovery challenges his resolve.

programme.title: English vinglish
programme.desc: Housewife And Caterer Shashi Is Usually Mocked By Her Family For Not Knowing English. Her Attempt To Learn The Language Helps Her Rediscover Herself And Reassert Her Value As A Mother And A Wife.

programme.title: Welcome To New York
programme.desc: An unexpected trip to New York City sets a duo off on a comic adventure that changes their lives forever.

programme.title: Sniff
programme.desc: An anosmic boy gains super smell powers and uses them to fight crime, turning into a pint-sized superhero with humor and noble purpose.

programme.title: Lost
programme.desc: When a young activist disappears under mysterious circumstances, a journalist embarks on a journey to unravel the truth behind the mystery.

programme.title: Saand Ki Aankh
programme.desc: Two elderly women become sharpshooting champions, defying age and gender norms to inspire a generation through grit and determination.

programme.title: Go Goa Gone
programme.desc: Three slackers face chaos on a zombie-infested island, blending horror and hilarity as they fight the undead with absurd tactics and plenty of dark humor.

programme.title: Bengal Tiger
programme.desc: Akash, A Simple Villager, Aspires To Become Famous After Being Rejected By A Girl. However, Things Get Complicated When He Hits The Agricultural Minister With A Stone.

programme.title: Phata Poster Nikla Hero
programme.desc: Vishwas Rao poses as an inspector for a photo shoot. Chaos erupts when Kajal mistakes him for a real inspector and his mother arrives, complicating matters.

programme.title: Masss
programme.desc: After his partner's death, a con artist begins interacting with ghosts. He encounters a vengeful spirit seeking justice for its family's killers, leading him into a dangerous and supernatural quest.

programme.title: Uri The Surgical Strike
programme.desc: The Indian army's special forces conduct a secret mission to retaliate against a terrorist organization that attacked their camp and killed their comrades.

programme.title: Police Power
programme.desc: Murugavel, a police officer, tries to reform his younger brother, who veers into crime. When unsuccessful, he confronts the local gangster fueling youth crime.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Learn about a wide selection of products and take advantage of a convenient method of shopping. A show for those who love to shop from the comforts of their home.

programme.title: Travels with Pevsner
programme.desc: Germaine Greer explores the architecture of Warwickshire with Nikolaus Pevsner's Buildings of England as her guide. Visits include Baddesley Clinton Hall and Warwick Castle.

programme.title: Rebuilding Notre-Dame
programme.desc: Five years after the devastating fire, Lucy Worsley has exclusive access to the rebuilding of the cathedral of Notre-Dame de Paris as the historic church enters the final stages of its restoration. Work on the complex roof and spire is nearing completion as restoration teams battle to be ready for the public reopening.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: Africa - home to the greatest wildlife gatherings on earth. But even in this land of plenty, wildlife faces huge challenges.

programme.title: The Latest Secrets of Hieroglyphs
programme.desc: The great history of Egypt is inscribed on its monuments, temples and tombs, but hieroglyphs – the written language of the ancient Egyptians – fell silent until 1822 when a young French scholar, Jean-François Champollion, became the first person to decipher their texts for over a thousand years. Champollion’s insights and the work of other scholars helped bring an entire civilisation back to life. Today, researchers are increasingly interested in the authors who created these hieroglyphic works. Near Luxor, The Latest Secrets of Hieroglyphs follows a new generation of Egyptologists as they unlock the texts inscribed inside a richly adorned tomb, revealing the beliefs and lives of the priests, scribes, painters, engravers and builders who created this grand funerary monument.

programme.title: Call My Bluff
programme.desc: Robert Robinson hosts as Patrick Campbell, Joanna Lumley and Lord Kearton take on Frank Muir, Pauline Yates and Donald Churchill in the game of word definitions and deceptions.

programme.title: Face the Music
programme.desc: Joseph Cooper invites viewers to match their musical wits against guests Annette Page, Richard Baker and Robin Ray. With guest musician Murray Perahia.

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: The team explore Nasa's Artemis II mission, the first crewed journey towards the moon since 1972. They also look into robotic landers and new scientific clues from lunar samples.

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: The incredible story of how Pluto has been propelled from an unremarkable ball of ice on the edge of the solar system to a world of unimaginable complexity - where some form of alien life might exist. Featuring first-hand accounts of the incredible discoveries made by New Horizons from many of the scientists involved in the mission.

programme.title: Art That Made Us
programme.desc: From the chaos of the Black Death comes creative renewal - survivors finding their voice through satire and a revived literature in English, including breakthrough works by women.

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Industrial Age
programme.desc: Fred Dibnah takes a look at mining, touring from Cornwall to Scotland to explore how tin, slate, lead and coal were all extracted from the ground. Along the way, he visits old mine workings, drives and winding engines, and he descends a mine shaft strapped to the top of the cage.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: The real side of boat life with Robbie Cumming. Robbie runs aground on the Rochdale Canal - will he make it to Manchester?

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth has arranged one of her candlelight suppers, planning to impress Emmet, the director of the local amateur operatic society, with her singing. But circumstances beyond her control soon take over the proceedings.

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: When Timothy's parents go on a second honeymoon, he plans to hold a party - with the intention of seducing Veronica Straddles.

programme.title: A History of Britain by Simon Schama
programme.desc: Queen Elizabeth I was one of the country's most intelligent monarchs, ruling a Protestant rogue state in a Catholic world. But it was her long, tangled relationship with her cousin Mary Queen of Scots that would test her the most. Elizabeth never married. Mary married twice but it ruined her. A magnet for conspiracy and intrigue, Mary tormented Elizabeth until finally executed for treason. But it was Mary not Elizabeth who gave birth to an heir. Simon Schama asks if it was the politician Elizabeth, or the mother Mary, who won in the end.

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: This two-part documentary is a personal journey by award-winning film-maker Ric Esther Bienstock that delves into the heart of the culture wars and freedom of speech conversations that have exploded on US campuses over the last ten years. Starting in 2017, the film hears from academics, activists and students on all sides of the divisive debates and paints a vivid picture of an educational environment marked by fear and threats. In this polarised academic landscape, Ric Esther Bienstock argues that the liberal ideals of open debate and diversity of thought are under siege.

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: The team explore Nasa's Artemis II mission, the first crewed journey towards the moon since 1972. They also look into robotic landers and new scientific clues from lunar samples.

programme.title: The Secret Life of Waves
programme.desc: Documentary-maker David Malone delves into the secrets of ocean waves. In an elegant and original film, he finds that waves are not made of water, that some waves travel sideways, and that the sound of the ocean comes not from water but from bubbles. Waves are not only beautiful but also profoundly important, and there is a surprising connection between the life cycle of waves and the life of human beings.

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Industrial Age
programme.desc: Fred Dibnah takes a look at mining, touring from Cornwall to Scotland to explore how tin, slate, lead and coal were all extracted from the ground. Along the way, he visits old mine workings, drives and winding engines, and he descends a mine shaft strapped to the top of the cage.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: The real side of boat life with Robbie Cumming. Robbie runs aground on the Rochdale Canal - will he make it to Manchester?

programme.title: Face the Music
programme.desc: Joseph Cooper invites viewers to match their musical wits against guests Annette Page, Richard Baker and Robin Ray. With guest musician Murray Perahia.

programme.title: The Secret Life of Waves
programme.desc: Documentary-maker David Malone delves into the secrets of ocean waves. In an elegant and original film, he finds that waves are not made of water, that some waves travel sideways, and that the sound of the ocean comes not from water but from bubbles. Waves are not only beautiful but also profoundly important, and there is a surprising connection between the life cycle of waves and the life of human beings.

programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Industrial Age
programme.desc: Fred Dibnah visits the North East, which is rich in railway history. At Bowes Railway, he sees an early engineering project by George Stephenson, which was a stationary engine that pulled coal wagons uphill with a rope. At Darlington Railway Museum he admires Stephenson's original Locomotive No 1, the first to run from Darlington to Stockton. At the National Railway Museum, York, he rides on a replica of the Rocket, made by Stephenson's son Robert, and at Ffestiniog Railway, he sees how a new locomotive is designed with computer aids and rides on the footplate and stokes the boiler of a Black Five at Llangollen Railway.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Life on board a narrowboat with Robbie Cumming. There are engine issues and a leaky boat to fix before Robbie reaches Liverpool Docks.

programme.title: What Are UFOs?
programme.desc: For decades, UFOs have intrigued the world, but many scientists avoided studying them. In the United States, after highly publicised sightings of unidentified objects by US Navy pilots, UFOs are moving out of the shadows and into the light as NASA pledges to study them scientifically. So what does science have to say? Though some are identified as balloons, drones, weather phenomena or optical illusions, others remain mysterious. Could they be the result of secret new technology? And what would it take for alien engineers to traverse vast distances to send probes or visit Earth from other solar systems? Explore the evidence as astrophysicists and engineers use new technologies to investigate the strangest objects in our skies.

programme.title: Talking Art: Cezanne
programme.desc: Celebrities talk about which of Cezanne's works they find most inspiring.

programme.title: Building Britain's Biggest Nuclear Power Station
programme.desc: In the second episode, our cameras pick up and follow the projects’ next major construction milestones. We get up close and personal with the world’s largest land-based crane during the biggest lift on the project to date. We learn how the team has created an extensive flood defence system to protect the site from the worst imaginable weather events - including a once-in-10,000 year storm surge - to avoid a nuclear disaster like the one that befell the Fukushima Daiichi power plant in Japan. We also follow specialists as they work to uncover unexploded World War II bombs that threaten the installation of the 4500-tonne cooling water intake heads.

programme.title: This Life
programme.desc: Warren falls in love, but when his romance turns sour he makes the decision not to live a lie. Meanwhile, Milly and O'Donnell are becoming increasingly close at work, and Jerry finds an unexpected ally in Anna.

programme.title: This Life
programme.desc: O'Donnell could prove to be Milly's knight in shining armour, but will she risk her relationship with Egg for a romantic fantasy? Meanwhile, Anna decides that an older man is just what she needs, and Miles deals with a flasher who claims to be a nudist.

programme.title: Catching History's Criminals: The Forensics Story
programme.desc: Gabriel Weston investigates the forensic advances that have elevated the murder weapon from its role of mere evidence to that of key witness.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Life on board a narrowboat with Robbie Cumming. There are engine issues and a leaky boat to fix before Robbie reaches Liverpool Docks.

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Industrial Age
programme.desc: Fred Dibnah visits the North East, which is rich in railway history. At Bowes Railway, he sees an early engineering project by George Stephenson, which was a stationary engine that pulled coal wagons uphill with a rope. At Darlington Railway Museum he admires Stephenson's original Locomotive No 1, the first to run from Darlington to Stockton. At the National Railway Museum, York, he rides on a replica of the Rocket, made by Stephenson's son Robert, and at Ffestiniog Railway, he sees how a new locomotive is designed with computer aids and rides on the footplate and stokes the boiler of a Black Five at Llangollen Railway.

programme.title: Enhanced Games: A Sporting Revolution or Dangerous Doping?
programme.desc: The Enhanced Games is one of sport's most controversial concepts. A multi-sport event where athletes, under medical supervision, are allowed to take performance enhancing drugs. The aim is to break world records in swimming, sprinting and weightlifting events for a total prize of 25 million dollars. There has been widespread criticism from sporting authorities and anti-doping bodies to the Games. BBC Sports Editor Dan Roan visits its training camp in Abu Dhabi.

programme.title: Joins BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.desc: In County Down, dairy farmer Derek Geddis turns to a volunteer search and rescue team after three of his cattle go missing. When the animals are found stranded in a river, the farmer’s skills and the team’s technology are put to the test in a delicate recovery mission. As daylight fades, the pressure mounts to bring the herd to safety. In County Durham, a community support officer’s drone footage helps catch deer poachers in the act. His quick response and aerial evidence lead to a rare conviction, highlighting how modern policing is tackling traditional countryside crimes. And in North Wales, reporter Paul Colgan investigates how police and scientists are using dog DNA to trace the animals behind devastating livestock attacks – a breakthrough that could bring long-awaited justice to farmers.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: In Scotland, Martel encounters a house with a safety order attached to it, which means certain rooms can’t be accessed. Owain is in south Wales to see a sizeable unit with lots of development potential. In Lincolnshire, Dion is doing all he can to escape the rain!

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The man who built a cinema at the bottom of his garden – it sounds like a Hollywood plot, but it’s true! Bus driver Andy Jones spent five years and more than £70,000 building the 30-seat movie theatre in Stoke-on-Trent. Andy began salvaging items from old cinemas when he was a boy, and today he hosts charity movie nights for his local community. Is there a Hollywood-style ending for either team in this episode? It’s sisters versus sisters at an antiques centre in Derbyshire. Karen and Claire are in red, while Sharon and Margaret are in blue. With expert Nick Hall by their side, the blues reflect on a fancy French mirror until Nick notices how modern it is. Meanwhile, the reds and Izzie Balmer ease themselves into their shop with a Glastonbury chair – but the price isn’t music to their ears. In Derby, auctioneer James Lewis sells the teams’ items, but what will the bidders make of them?

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Shift the Thrift
programme.desc: In Hastings, partners James and Elaine scour 15 charity shops, hunting for vintage gems and a hidden star buy before facing a tense, live online auction for charity.

programme.title: Designing the Hebrides
programme.desc: Banjo has been commissioned by fish shop manager and friend Sally, who wants to modernise her family’s traditional business in Tobermory. Banjo has bold plans to utterly transform both the inside and outside of the shop, but Sally is worried that her family will disapprove of the radical makeover. With his band of trusty locals, including Eoghann the carpenter and handyman Tom, Banjo takes huge risks in realising his design ambitions - but how will the family feel about the new look?

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: It’s all go in rural Oxfordshire as Richie Anderson helps a couple branch out and leave the suburbs to fulfil their dream of creating a forest school. With an impressive budget, the stunning property line-up is full of leafy promise, including a beautiful, converted mill house that’s anything but run-of-the-mill. Richie also meets an innovative young artist who creates her masterpieces on a bicycle-powered sewing machine... artistry on a ‘wheelie’ different level!

programme.title: I Made It at Market
programme.desc: After giving each of them money-making plans at his business bootcamp, Dom finds out how upholsterer Hayley from Hampshire and stone carver Richard from Northumberland have transformed their lives and what they are earning now. Hayley arrived at the bootcamp in the summer of 2023 looking for help to build a business for her and husband Martin, who has a chronic back condition. Now, Dom drops in on their home workshop to discover how the couple are achieving their dream and the fantastic amount of money they are making from their work. In Northumberland, Richard reveals how the guidance he received at the bootcamp in the summer of 2024 has changed his life and how much his stone carving earns him now.

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp, with sidekick Sarah Greene, hosts the quiz show where contestants race in moving podiums across the studio floor. Correct answers get the players moving, while wrong answers will stop them in their tracks. Five contestants – Lee, Nadine, Tom, Carol and Albert – battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple – make it to the finish line before your rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the £5,000 cash prize.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Tom Allen for the brainy quiz where the contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: Party Election Broadcasts: Reform UK
programme.desc: A party election broadcast by Reform UK for the local and mayoral elections on 7 May.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: It’s the day of Vicki and Ross’s stag dos, Jasmine starts to feel part of the Slaters, and Bea makes a decision about her love life.

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: President Trump’s crackdown on immigration has led to a big drop in the number of migrants illegally crossing the border and a sharp rise in arrests in US cities. But his administration’s hardline approach has divided America. Two protesters have been shot dead on the streets of Minneapolis by federal agents, and an estimated 14 million undocumented immigrants are at risk of deportation. Reporter Darragh MacIntyre meets some of the families living under threat and asks whether the president’s tough policies are working.

programme.title: Trace, Track, Get My Car Back
programme.desc: In Felixstowe, ports intelligence officer Adam and his colleagues intercept hundreds of stolen vehicles before they are smuggled out of the country. Today, they find a suspect shipping container crammed with stolen cars. One of the vehicles, a Toyota RAV4, is hoisted up at the back of the container and has to be removed carefully. It looks like a well-loved family car with a child seat and several children’s toys, including a teddy bear, on the back seat. Adam and his team contact the owners so the children can be reunited with their toys. Successes like this help motivate them in the fight against organised car crime. There is also shocking footage of thieves stealing another family car - a Range Rover - from a driveway in Essex. It takes them less than a minute to break in using a reprogrammed key. The car is fitted with a tracker, but the signal goes dead, and the car disappears without trace. It is later found at Tilbury Docks, wedged inside a shipping container. Meanwhile, tracker Richard is searching for a heavy-duty roller stolen from a construction site in Bristol. The roller is fitted with a tiny tracker, and after a lengthy search, Richard finds it hidden inside a van.

programme.title: Babies
programme.desc: Lisa tries to find a way of getting Stephen to talk to her. Amanda splits up with Dave before beginning the move to her new life.

programme.title: Tinker Tailor Soldier Spy
programme.desc: Film adaptation of John Le Carre's Cold War novel about a retired MI6 agent who must track down a Soviet mole within the British intelligence service's ranks.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.desc: In Lincolnshire, rising floodwater sweeps through low-lying land, forcing farmers to step in to protect their community. Father and son Dean and Ryan Wesley join the emergency response, using their machinery and local knowledge to help save lives. But when the flood reaches their own family, the pair launch a daring rescue to bring their relatives to safety. On farmland in Wiltshire, investigators uncover one of Britain’s most shocking cases of bird crime, after a tip-off leads to a secret camera catching a suspect in the act. As teams sift through horrific evidence of dead raptors, the scale of what could be the UK’s largest bird of prey persecution becomes clear. And in Northern Ireland, reporter Paul Colgan joins the Mourne Mountain Rescue Team to discover the challenges they overcome to keep hill farmers safe in some of the country’s toughest terrain.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin comes across a layout he’s never seen before, which is rare given his 20-plus years on the show! Dion encounters all weathers in Manchester and Leeds.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents today's show from Oswestry in Shropshire. With the help of experts Charles Hanson and Ben Cooper, two teams compete to buy the most profitable items to sell at auction. In north Wales, Charlie meets sculptor Simon O’Rourke, who produces pieces of artwork using waste timber or standing stumps and a variety of power and hand tools.

programme.title: Shift the Thrift
programme.desc: In High Wycombe, mother and daughter Liz and Alice battle to raise money for Great Ormond Street Hospital, gambling on vintage fashion, ceramics and a bold sewing machine buy.

programme.title: Designing the Hebrides
programme.desc: A short ferry ride from the Isle of Mull lies Ulva, a tiny island with a handful of residents. Banjo has been asked to update a small bothy which the islanders want to rent out to visitors. With no power, water or easy access, Banjo has his work cut out. However, with the help of handyman Tom, painter Lisa and her husband Ro, he embarks on his most ambitious design brief to date. Can he turn this tumbledown shelter into a cosy, inviting retreat?

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple who have never lived in an older home but who are now ready to fall for a bit of period charm in either Norfolk or Suffolk. With a £270,000 budget and three lively whippets to please, they are after a dog-friendly garden and a slice of East Anglian architectural history to call home. Alistair also meets a willow farmer and discovers how this wonderfully sustainable crop is being woven into the future.

programme.title: I Made It at Market
programme.desc: Dom catches up with metal sculptor Lindon and keepsake artist Andie from Devon, who have made serious money from their creative passions. Lindon came to Dom’s business boot camp. looking for help to build a dream family business in the summer of 2023. Now, Dom drops into his fantastic workshop to find out how Lindon has made his dream come true and the amazing amount of money he is earning from his work. In Devon, Andie reveals how Dom and mentor Sonny Bailey-Aird’s support and guidance have helped her to build a life-changing business since she came to the boot camp in the summer of 2024 and how much money she is making now.

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.desc: In Northern Ireland, farmer Mark Mahon is in despair as 12 of his sheep become stranded on rocks in Strangford Lough, one of the most dangerous tidal waters in the UK. With the tide rising fast and strong currents closing in, the RNLI and coastguard launch their most challenging rescue operation to date. In North Wales, a wildlife photographer captures evidence of a badger sett under attack. Quick thinking sets off an RSPCA and police investigation that uncovers a serious case of wildlife crime. And in South Yorkshire, reporter Paul Colgan joins the police Quad Squad as they tackle illegal off-road riding and the growing damage it causes to rural communities.

programme.title: Designing the Hebrides
programme.desc: Banjo and his team head north to the Isle of Skye and their biggest transformation to date. Owners of a remote cafe and bookshop are desperate to update their business, but with a vast space to fill, Banjo takes the bold decision to spend almost all his budget on a set of huge stained-glass windows. Far from home, and with tricky logistics, will this job push the team to their limits, and will Banjo’s ambitious gamble pay off with his clients?

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Richie Anderson joins the show's line-up as he helps a couple of born-and-bred Brummies to find the perfect character property on his home turf of the rural Midlands. A turnkey, ready-to-move-into home is high on the priority list for our busy buyers, one of whom is a primary school head teacher. But rebel Richie happily runs the risk of detention when he serves up a mystery house requiring a little extra love and attention. Continuing on his maiden voyage, Richie takes a break from property to skipper a narrowboat as he takes to the water in a bid to learn all about slow tourism.

programme.title: I Made It at Market
programme.desc: Dom checks in on digital artist Tasia and pole lathe woodturner Shannon to hear about their stunning achievements and how much their skills are earning them after visiting his transformational business boot camp in the spring of 2022. Retail worker Tasia dreamt of becoming a full-time artist, and Dom finds out about her jaw-dropping success and the amount of money she is now making that is helping to turn that dream into a reality. Shannon reveals how broadening her business is helping her to build a long-term creative career and how much she is making from it.

programme.title: UEFA Champions League
programme.desc: Highlights from the quarter-final second legs in the UEFA Champions League. Premier League leaders Arsenal welcome Sporting CP to London and Bayern Munich face Real Madrid. There are also highlights from Tuesday’s matches, with Liverpool taking on the defending champions PSG and Athletico Madrid hosting Barcelona. Kelly Cates presents with analysis from Stephen Warnock and Nedum Onuoha.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents the high-stakes quiz, where in this edition, a team of athletes - Kriss Akabusi, Sammi Kinghorn, Matt Baker and Gladiator Karenjeet Kaur Bains - get together to take on the Bridge of Lies for their chosen charities. One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each Bridge crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and ten lies. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer. Step on a lie, and the Bridge will halve their money. Step on three lies, and they are out of the game. There is always a safe path across the Bridge. They just have to find it. Once they have each faced the Bridge on their own, those who have been successful will join forces to face the final crossing for a chance to take home the cash for their chosen charities.

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: The legendary Blankety Blank is back! Bradley Walsh hosts as a panel of six celebrities fill in the missing blanks and help win brilliant prizes for some very lucky contestants. This week, he’s joined by Richard Ayoade, Alex Jones, Stephen Bailey, Nadia Jae, Gyles Brandreth and Roisin Conaty.

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Tir goes even further into the underbelly of Las Vegas to investigate how the reinvention of the city to pull in a new generation of visitors is affecting those who live and work there.

programme.title: Behind Bars: Sex, Bribes and Murder
programme.desc: An investigation into a viral prison sex tape leads journalist Mobeen Azhar to discover an illicit prison economy fuelled by inappropriate relationships, coercion and contraband.

programme.title: Behind Bars: Sex, Bribes and Murder
programme.desc: Mobeen’s investigation into prison corruption hears of the murder of a former officer who had been involved in uncovering an illicit relationship between a guard and an inmate.

programme.title: Press X on the BBC
programme.desc: Eight games, a book and a luxury. What would you choose? Steffan Powell chats with gaming YouTuber Elz the Witch and gaming experts and comedians Inel Tomlinson and Glenn Moore. Like the programme? Listen to more on the Press X To Continue podcast, available on BBC Sounds.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall delves into the weird and wonderful world of parasites in this Deadly 60 special. From leeches to tapeworms and cuckoos to mosquitoes he reveals the mind-boggling methods that parasites employ to survive, breed and feed by exploiting their animal and human hosts.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: This time, Steve and the crew travel to Venezuela in search of more lethal animal predators for their Deadly list. First, they comb the Venezuelan wetlands looking for the biggest snake in the world - the mighty green anaconda, capable of growing to over six metres in length. Then Steve heads beneath the rainforest into a dark cave infested with vampire bats, where a true toxic terror lurks - the scolopendra, a giant centipede armed with a powerful venomous bite.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy, Richard and James attempt to save the planet by building their own electric car and then put their latest 'green' creation through the rigours of the new car testing process. Meanwhile on the track, it is the battle of the continents as Europe takes on the USA and Jeremy pits the Corvette ZR1's muscle against the finesse of Audi's R8 V10. The Star in a Reasonably Priced Car is Frost/Nixon and The Damned United star Michael Sheen.

programme.title: Louis Theroux's LA Stories
programme.desc: Los Angeles is home to more than a quarter of a million dogs - from the pampered pooches of Beverley Hills to the pit bull terriers of the projects. Louis Theroux enters the bizarre world of LA's dogs, meeting the characters whose lives revolve around the city's huge canine population. In the toughest neighbourhoods of south LA, he has a terrifying encounter with a 'weaponised' dog, trained to attack on command, and goes on patrol with 'Dogman', whose mission is to rescue some of the thousands of stray and feral pit bulls roaming the streets. At the city's biggest animal shelter, Louis meets the hard-pressed staff, whose heartbreaking job includes euthanizing thousands of unwanted dogs every year. He also encounters the middle-class Americans who adopt some of these delinquent dogs, as well as the colourful dog-training gurus who claim to be able to take the ghetto out of the dog once the dog has been rescued from the streets.

programme.title: Louis Theroux's LA Stories
programme.desc: Louis visits LA's most famous hospital, the Cedars-Sinai Medical Centre in West Hollywood, where he meets patients battling for their lives against serious illnesses. America spends a huge amount on health care and a hefty proportion goes on end of life care, with a wide and expensive array of treatments on offer to those with insurance or who qualify for government funded programmes. Louis talks to patients and their families as they face some of the toughest decisions imaginable - whether to accept death and die in relative comfort, or to gamble on further, possibly painful treatment in the hope that it will cure them or prolong their life. He also talks to the doctors whose job it is to guide people through this traumatic process. Over several months Louis explores the American way of death, which often involves never giving up and keeping faith right to the end.

programme.title: Louis Theroux's LA Stories
programme.desc: In the final episode of Louis Theroux's LA Stories, Louis spends time in the twilight world of the city's paroled sex offenders, whose crimes have made them pariahs living on the fringes of society. The State of California has some of the toughest regulations anywhere in America controlling sex offenders. On leaving prison they can spend years on parole, tagged and tracked, and a lifetime on an online sex offenders register. Louis spent several months at some of the hostels in Los Angeles where sex offenders live, talking in depth to sex offenders themselves as well as the people charged with monitoring them. The tough rules governing where they can live and who they can see make it hard for registered sex offenders to find work or even housing, and many are forced into homelessness. Meeting these outcasts Louis asks whether these tough rules keep society safer and when, if ever, it is right to give sex offenders a second chance.

programme.title: High
programme.desc: Aged 19 and bored of where she grew up, Michaella craved sun and adventure. But her dream turned into a nightmare as she fell into an illicit world of drugs and excess.

programme.title: High
programme.desc: Michaella tells the story of finding herself 5,000 miles from home in Peru, pretending to be a tourist at Machu Picchu, then walking 12kg of cocaine into an airport.

programme.title: High
programme.desc: Michaella describes how she walked into Lima airport with a suitcase full of cocaine, hoping that everything she had been told about how easy it would be was true.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve returns to South America to search for more of the planet's deadliest animals. He joins a team trying to save a highly endangered predator, the orinoco crocodile, and witnesses the ferocious maternal instincts of a female crocodile protecting her nest. In Brazil, he heads out for a night-time encounter with a metre-long monster of the Amazonian waterways, the wolf fish, and back on dry land, Steve turns to technology in an attempt to track down one of South America's most elusive jungle predators, the enigmatic jaguar.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall and the crew head for the island paradise of Sri Lanka in search of two massive predators. Steve attempts to free dive with the largest animal ever known to have lived - the blue whale. Back on dry land, he goes in search of another giant, the Asian elephant. Along the way, Steve and the crew also encounter two very deadly snakes, a highly venomous Russell's viper and an enormous, bone-crushing Indian rock python.

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: Leaving the tranquillity of Kefalonia behind, the teams race through Greece to reach one of Europe’s biggest and most vibrant cities, Istanbul, where Europe and Asia collide.

programme.title: High
programme.desc: Michaella describes the dilemma she faced: stick to her story about being forced to carry the drugs and face 15 years in jail, or come clean and break her mother’s heart.

programme.title: High
programme.desc: Despite being sentenced to nearly seven years in a maximum security prison, Michaella was home in less than three. She reveals how she got out and how prison changed her for the better.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Animated sketch comedy set on a seemingly quiet residential street where the neighbours are all just trying to get through the day without incident. But beneath the calm exterior, everyday life quickly unravels into the absurd, the awkward and the unexpectedly hilarious - from dodging a secret crush to desperately trying to impress the builder. Whether it’s a piano teacher adapting to a four-legged student, a cycling enthusiast battling to stay inside his own outfit, or a work-from-home mum constantly thrown off course, life on the street is anything but peaceful. Blending sharp observational humour with bursts of slapstick chaos, the show transforms suburban routine into a playground of comic mayhem - where absolutely anything can happen.
